database.parallel_safety: low (single-file lock; tests must serialize or use isolated fixtures)

auth.dev_backdoor_behavior: token=='dev' bypasses JWT decode and resolves to first user in DB; tests should NOT use this — use real login.

default_admin.seeded_when: first startup if user table is empty

user_role.name: 普通用户
user_role.scope: all menus + GET-only apis + 基础模块 tag apis

preferred_role_for_e2e_assignment.name: 普通用户
preferred_role_for_e2e_assignment.rationale: Stable seed name; non-default-admin role prevents accidental privilege escalation in tests; clearly distinguishable from the role used by tests' admin login.

list_user.search_semantics: icontains for username and email; AND combined
list_user.response_schema: SuccessExtra: {code:200, msg:null, data:[user...], total:int, page:int, page_size:int}

get_user.missing_id_behavior: raises Tortoise DoesNotExist → caught by DoesNotExistHandle → returns 404 with body {code:404, msg:'Object has not found, exc: ..., query_params: ...'}
get_user.response_schema_ok: Success: {code:200, msg:'OK', data:{...user dict without password}}

create_user.body_schema: UserCreate: {email: EmailStr (required), username: str (required), password: str (required), is_active: bool (default true), is_superuser: bool (default false), role_ids: List[int] (default []), dept_id: Optional[int] (default 0)}

body.msg: The user with this email already exists in the system.

duplicate_email_response.note: Fail(code=400, ...) returns HTTP 400 with matching body code.

missing_required_field_response.note: FastAPI Pydantic validation handled by RequestValidationHandle.

side_effects: User row created with password hashed via app.utils.password.get_password_hash
user.roles m2m populated via update_roles(user, role_ids) which CLEARS then ADDs

update_user.body_schema: UserUpdate: {id: int (required), email: EmailStr (required), username: str (required), is_active: bool (default true), is_superuser: bool (default false), role_ids: List[int] (default []), dept_id: int (default 0)}
update_user.missing_id_behavior: controller calls self.update(id=...) which uses get(id=...) → DoesNotExist → DoesNotExistHandle → 404
update_user.role_update_semantics: update_roles() does roles.clear() then add(role_id) for each id in role_ids; result is total replacement (no merge with existing roles)
update_user.important_caveat: update_user does NOT re-hash password (no password field in UserUpdate). Update path mutates only fields present in UserUpdate.

body.msg: 不允许重置超级管理员密码

exception_handlers.DoesNotExist: 404 + {code:404, msg:'Object has not found, exc: ..., query_params: ...'}

TC-USER-051.deferred_value: 默认超级管理员的内部 ID
TC-USER-051.resolved_to: id of user where username='admin' and is_superuser=true
TC-USER-051.expected_msg: 不允许重置超级管理员密码

TC-USER-103.rationale: Pre-seeded; not the role of the logged-in admin (avoids data ambiguity); name is stable across DB resets.

creation_methods: Preferred for tests: ORM directly via app.models.admin.User + get_password_hash for fast setup
Alternative: API call to POST /api/v1/user/create (slower, exercises full stack — better for E2E preconditions if test wants to exercise the contract)
cleanup_methods: ORM: User.filter(username__in=[...]).delete() (also cascades roles m2m via Tortoise) — use in pytest fixture teardown
uniqueness_constraints: email (business-level check via get_by_email; no DB unique constraint enforced — Fail(400) only)
test_data_factories.warning_email_business_uniqueness: Email duplicate check is implemented in handler (get_by_email + Fail(400)), NOT a DB unique index. Concurrent inserts could theoretically race, but for sequential test execution this is reliable.

superuser_admin.effect: All /api/v1/user/* endpoints accessible without role-based api binding.

non_superuser_admin_role_user.permission_source: User.roles → Role.apis → list of (method, path) tuples
non_superuser_admin_role_user.effect: Has full /api/v1/user/* access via 管理员 role binding.

non_superuser_user_role_user.permission_source: Filtered: GET methods + tags='基础模块'

e2e_navigation_facts.menu_path: 系统管理 → 用户管理
e2e_navigation_facts.menu_seeded_in: init_menus() — parent=系统管理 (path=/system, redirect=/system/user), child=用户管理 (path=user, component=/system/user)

test_isolation_strategy.shared_resource: single sqlite file db.sqlite3 — no transactional isolation across tests
guidance: Use unique usernames per test (timestamp suffix or uuid hex) to avoid cross-test bleed
Use try/finally or pytest fixture teardown to delete created users
Avoid mutating seeded roles (管理员, 普通用户) — only assign/unassign them
test_isolation_strategy.parallel_test_safety: DO NOT run tests in parallel against the same db.sqlite3 — sqlite write contention + shared seed state will cause flaky tests.

known_constraints_for_planning: API token header name is `token`, NOT `Authorization`. Plan headers must reflect this.
Login response data path is `data.access_token`. Plan must extract correctly.
Default admin is superuser → all endpoints accessible without role-binding setup. Tests don't need to grant API permissions explicitly.
reset_password fixed value is `123456`. Re-login validation must use this constant.
DoesNotExist → 404 (not 400). Negative cases TC-USER-011/031/041/052 should expect HTTP 404 with body code=404.
RequestValidationError → 422. Validation case TC-USER-022 should expect HTTP 422 with body code=422.
Duplicate email → HTTP 400 with body {code:400, msg: 'The user with this email already exists in the system.'}. TC-USER-021 should expect this.
Superuser block → HTTP 403 with body {code:403, msg:'不允许重置超级管理员密码'}. TC-USER-051 should expect this.
update_roles is destructive (clear+add). TC-USER-030 / TC-USER-103 must verify ONLY the new role exists post-update.
Update endpoint does not modify password field — only the fields in UserUpdate schema (note: UserUpdate has no password field at all).
User.to_dict(m2m=True) is used in list endpoint — list response includes roles populated; get_user response uses to_dict() without m2m=True so roles may be empty array there.
